titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Frederick Samuel Dellenbaugh Journal and Lantern Slides from the Harriman Alaska Expedition
Dellenbaugh (Frederick Samuel) Harriman Alaska Expedition Journal and Lantern Slides
titlestmt.author: Stephanie Bredbenner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday April 13, 2026 at 23:25

did.unittitle: Frederick Samuel Dellenbaugh journal and lantern slides from the Harriman Alaska Expedition

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Journal, ephemera, glass lantern slides, and glass plate negatives documenting Frederick Samuel Dellenbaugh's participation in the Harriman Alaska Expedition of 1899.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dellenbaugh's holograph journal of the Harriman Alaska Expedition, 23 May-9 August 1899, kept from his departure from New York City until his return. A detailed diary recording daily activities of the expedition, interviews with natives and recent settlers, scientific findings, and discussions with others on the expedition. There are small illustrations by Dellenbaugh throughout, and at the back there are signatures and addresses of expedition members and poems written on the trip. The journal is accompanied by clippings, programs, photographs, maps, and letters from E. H. Harriman, Robert Swain Gifford, and Lewis R. Morris. The 115 lantern slides document the Harriman Alaska Expedition and were created by Dellenbaugh, C. Hart Merriam, and G. K. Gilbert. The slides depict a Tlinkit summer camp at Yakutat Bay, buildings at Sitka, Muir Glacier, Disenchantment Bay, Prince William Sound, St. Paul Island seal rookeries, whale rib houses, dog teams, a Kodiak village, a lifeboat drill on board the G.W. Elder, men gathering ice from the sea, igloo building, and gold miners working. Accompanied by 4 glass plate negatives of Alaska. Also present are a small quantity of lantern slides relating to Alaska, circa 1916, which Dellenbaugh requested from the American Museum of Natural History.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Frederick Samuel Dellenbaugh, artist, topographer, explorer and author, member of John Wesley Powell's second expedition down the Colorado River, and a member of the Harriman Alaska Expedition in 1899.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research. Boxes 4 and 5: Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. For further information consult the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Frederick Samuel Dellenbaugh Journal and Associated Lantern Slides from the Harriman Alaska Expedition. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was previously described with a short catalog record. A retroactive finding aid was created in 2026 to facilitate description and access to the collection at the folder level.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Dellenbaugh's original titles are encloed in quotation marks; otherwise, titles were devised by the archivist. Some folder titles contain language which may be inaccurate and harmful to Alaska Natives. Original titles have been maintained to preserve the original context of how the collection creator labeled their documents. Please contact Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library for details or if you have questions or comments about these revisions. For more information on reparative archival description at Yale, see Yale's Statement on Harmful Language in Archival Description.

did.unittitle: Journal of the Harriman Alaska Expedition
Contact prints for glass plate negatives in Box 5
"Yakutat Bay Tlinkit Summer Camp H.A.E. Book days Glacier Bay"
"Alaska - Juneau - Expedition ship G.W. Elder at pier"
"Glacial water running in a canyon of ice, Muir Glacier"
"Children (Yakutat) skinning a seal. Summer camp, Disenchantment Bay"
"Hubbard Glacier, Disenchantment Bay - beginning of Russell Fjord"
"Ice cascade near the front of the Nunatak Glacier - Nunatak inlet - Russell Fjord"
"Looking back on steamer's wake coming out of Russell Fjord, Hubbard Glacier in sight"
"The deserted village, Cape Fox, Alaska, showing carved post"
"Life belt drill on approaching Prince William Sound"
"Wellesley Glacier, College Fjord, Prince William Sound"
"Head of College Fjord, Port Wells, Prince William Sound. Harvard and Yale glaciers"
"Family of fur seals on St. Paul Island, Bering Sea"
"On Hall Island, Bering Sea - botanist in mid distance"
"Part of whale rib and sod house, Plover Bay"
"Junction of the Fish and the [Niukluk] rivers, Alaska"

did.unittitle: "Dog teams towing a boat around a gravel spit - Fish River, Alaska"
"Steamer on Fish River at White Mountain settlement, Alaska"
"Military prisoners hauling gravel at Nome, Alaska"
"Council City, Alaska on [Niukluk] River 60 miles above Golovin Bay"
"Exterior of Van der Burgh + Co.'s cabin at Council City"
"Interior of Van der Burgh + Co.'s cabin, Council City"
Unidentified landscape with two men in the middle distance
"Prospecting on Warner Creek, branch of the [Niukluk]"
Unidentified landscape with small boats in foreground
"Shoshone Falls from the north brink of canyon"
"Natives cutting out blocks of snow and building an igloo, Eskimo"
